source_title: 02 Building Environments/02 What are PBR Materials

evidences.content: PBR stands for Physically Based Rendering, a standard approach to materials.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: PBR materials have three core properties: Base Color, Roughness, and Metalness.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: Base Color defines the albedo of the surface without lighting.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: Roughness controls how smooth or rough a surface appears, affecting specular reflections.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: Metalness determines if a surface behaves like a metal or a dielectric.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: Metals have colored specular reflections and no diffuse. Dielectrics have white specular.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: UE5 uses the Microfacet BRDF model for realistic surface rendering.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: Example: A rusty metal pipe has high roughness but full metalness for a weathered look.
evidences.speaker: instructor

evidences.content: In Unreal, you create materials in the Material Editor using node-based graph.
evidences.speaker: instructor
